# HAL Golden Test Data

This directory contains known input/output pairs for verifying HAL compression/decompression behavior.

## Purpose

Golden test data allows CI to verify that:
1. Mock HAL produces deterministic, consistent output
2. Real HAL (when available) produces expected compression results
3. Mock behavior matches the recorded real HAL behavior

## Files

- `checksums.json` - SHA-256 checksums of inputs and expected outputs
- `test_*_input.bin` - Raw input data (generated by test regeneration)

## How to Regenerate

Run with real exhal/inhal binaries:

```bash
SPRITEPAL_EXHAL_PATH=/path/to/exhal SPRITEPAL_INHAL_PATH=/path/to/inhal \
    uv run pytest tests/test_hal_golden.py --regenerate-golden -v
```

This will:
1. Generate test input data patterns
2. Compress each pattern with real HAL
3. Record checksums in `checksums.json`

## Verification

Tests marked `@pytest.mark.golden_hal` verify mock output consistency.
Tests marked `@pytest.mark.real_hal` verify real HAL matches recorded checksums.

This allows CI to verify mock behavior without requiring real HAL binaries.

## Test Patterns

| Pattern | Size | Description |
|---------|------|-------------|
| zeros | 4KB | All zeros (highly compressible) |
| ones | 4KB | All 0xFF bytes |
| pattern | 8KB | Repeating byte pattern |
| small | ~300B | Small test string |
| medium | 4KB | Single repeated byte |
| large | 32KB | Single repeated byte |
